paper_title: Material and methods of fossil collection and analysis in Most Basin

methods.description: Process for collecting and preparing fish fossils from the Most Basin for study, including drying and preservation techniques.

steps.description: Fossils were collected dominantly in the Merkur Mine and selected relevant specimens from the National Museum's collections.
steps.searchable_summary: collect fish fossils from Merkur Mine and National Museum collections

steps.description: The claystone rocks with fossils were dried very slowly, to eliminate cracking caused by volume changes.
steps.searchable_summary: slowly dry claystone rocks with fossils to prevent cracking

steps.description: Some specimens, especially in the carbon rich claystones, were infused and glued with consolidant Paraloid B-72.
steps.searchable_summary: infuse and glue carbon-rich claystone specimens with Paraloid B-72

methods.description: Analysis of the stratigraphic position of fossil specimens using geochemical, mineralogical, and palaeomagnetic data.

steps.description: Discovery of crandallite layers and the newest research about geochemistry, mineralogical composition, palaeomagnetic dating and correlations in Most Basin helped to identify the stratigraphic position of new specimens.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze geochemical, mineralogical, and palaeomagnetic data to determine stratigraphic positions
